phase1.output:
Inner: Hello from Outer
Inner: Static message
Outer method called
---
Static Nested: Static message
---
Local inner class
---
Anonymous inner class running
phase1.explanation:
Inner classes are classes defined inside other classes:

1. Non-static Inner Class (Inner Class):
• Has access to outer class members (even private)
• Needs an outer instance: outer.new InnerClass()
• Can access instance variables and static variables

2. Static Nested Class:
• Can't access non-static outer members
• Doesn't need outer instance: new Outer.StaticNestedClass()

3. Local Inner Class:
• Defined inside a method
• Only accessible inside that method

4. Anonymous Inner Class:
• No name, defined on the fly
• Used for interfaces and abstract classes
• Common with event handlers

When to use:
• Inner class: when it's tightly coupled with outer
• Static nested: when it doesn't need outer instance
• Anonymous: for one-time use (GUI listeners)

phase2.errorMessage:
Outer.java:7: error: non-static variable message cannot be referenced from a static context
            System.out.println(message);  // ERROR!
                               ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Static nested class can't access non-static members of the outer class.

message is an instance variable — it belongs to an Outer object. Since Inner is static, it doesn't have a reference to an Outer instance.

Fixes:
1. Make message static: static String message = "Hello";
2. Make Inner non-static: class Inner
3. Pass an instance: System.out.println(outer.message);

Remember: static nested classes are like top-level classes but packaged inside another class.

phase3.prompt:
Create a class Library with:
• Private field: name (String)
• Inner class: Book with fields title, author
• Method in Library: addBook(String title, String author) that creates a Book
• Method: listBooks() that prints all books

In main, create a Library, add 2 books, and list them.
dialogueHints: Library is the outer class, Book is the inner class
Book should have a constructor taking title and author
Store books in an ArrayList<Book> inside Library
